strategy: He resists Balance 50% and takes only +5% from Myth/Life/Death, so use a strong off-Balance hit or a prism rather than Balance.
He summons a Beastman Guard at the end of every round equal to your team size and re-summons them, so do not waste turns clearing minions, race him with AoE.
Use Mass Feint (+75%) instead of a regular Feint. A normal Feint makes him Swap Ward the traps onto you, Mass Feint does not trigger it. Indemnity or Melt also protects a single-target Feint.
Any defeat, ally or enemy, gives him Berserk and more damage, so avoid picking off minions right before your hit, kill him in the same burst.
Stack Mass Feint plus blades, then land one large off-school hit, and watch him drain 2 pips from allies to power up.
cheatNotes: Join after the duel starts and he hits you with Savage Paw.
He summons a Beastman Guard at the end of every round via a Moon spell.
He keeps minions equal to your team size and re-summons one per round until they match, do not over-invest in clearing them.
He steals 400 health from one of his minions and gives himself a Balanceblade.
He also interrupt-casts Chromatic Blast on himself, scaled to his pips, buffing his damage.
Every few rounds he drains 2 pips from each minion and converts them to power pips, so he ramps quickly, end the fight before he snowballs.
Place a single-target Feint and he Swap-Wards it onto you. Use Mass Feint or protect it instead.
Indemnity or Melt stops the Feint swap, pack one to trap him safely.
Mass Feint (+75%) does not trigger the swap, so it is the safe way to trap him.
Any defeat, ally or enemy, gives him Berserk and more damage, burst him down rather than trading kills.
